proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Medicine Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite sports medicine model linking knee, shoulder, elbow, and foot-ankle regional hubs.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Knee Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Knee ligament, meniscus, extensor, and articular structures for sports knee conditions.
Primary medial knee stabilizer resisting valgus stress.
Primary lateral knee stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Posterolateral knee stabilizing complex including LCL, popliteus, and arcuate ligaments.
C-shaped medial meniscus distributing load and resisting shear at the medial compartment.
More mobile lateral meniscus with popliteus hiatus; commonly torn with ACL injuries.
Hyaline cartilage covering femoral condyles and tibial plateau.
Medial and lateral femoral condyles forming the proximal tibiofemoral articulation.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Shoulder Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Glenohumeral stabilizers, rotator cuff, and biceps anchor for shoulder sports conditions.
Scapular articular surface forming the socket of the glenohumeral joint.
Fibrocartilaginous rim deepening the glenoid and anchoring capsulolabral structures.
Supraspinatus, infraspinatus, subscapularis, and teres minor force-couple complex.
Superior rotator cuff tendon initiating abduction and commonly torn in cuff pathology.
Posterior cuff tendon providing external rotation strength.
Anterior cuff tendon providing internal rotation and anterior stability.
Inferior posterior cuff muscle contributing to external rotation.
Superior labrum and biceps anchor complex at the glenoid origin.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Elbow Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Elbow ligament and tendon structures relevant to throwing and flexion injuries.
Medial elbow stabilizer critical in throwing athletes.
Lateral elbow stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Distal biceps insertion on the radial tuberosity enabling elbow flexion and supination.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Foot & Ankle Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Lateral ankle ligaments, syndesmosis, Achilles, and hindfoot structures for sports foot-ankle conditions.
Primary lateral ankle stabilizer injured in inversion sprains.
Lateral ankle ligament stabilizing subtalar motion after inversion injury.
Conjoined gastrocnemius-soleus tendon inserting on the calcaneus.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Lower Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Cross-cluster LE trauma anatomy hub reused for sports knee conditions.
Primary knee stabilizer in ACL tear and multiligament injuries.
Posterior knee stabilizer in PCL and multiligament injuries.
Sesamoid in extensor mechanism relevant to patellar instability.
Proximal tibial articular surface shared with tibial plateau fracture neighborhood.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage in patellar instability.
Distal femoral condylar region shared with trauma neighborhoods.
Vascular structure at risk in knee dislocation and multiligament injury.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in multiligament and posterolateral injuries.
Anterior cruciate ligament tear causing rotational knee instability.
Educational classification framework for acl tear.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: ACL Tear MRI Pattern

metadata.description: Characteristic MRI findings in acl tear.
Operative reconstruction concept for acl tear.
Return-to-play rehabilitation milestones for acl tear.
Common post-injury complication in acl tear.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: ACL Tear Physical Exam

metadata.description: Key physical examination maneuver for acl tear.
Cross-neighborhood reference to canonical condition meniscus-tear.
Cross-neighborhood reference to canonical condition patellar-instability.
Cross-neighborhood reference to canonical condition multiligament-knee-injury.
Cross-neighborhood reference to canonical condition tibial-plateau-fracture.

metadata.claim_text: ACL tears are instability injuries diagnosed by history and exam, with reconstruction decisions shaped by goals and associated pathology.
ACL Tear pathoanatomy centers on acl, medial-meniscus, lateral-meniscus and related stabilizers.
Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating acl tear.
Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for acl tear — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.
State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for acl tear.
Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for acl tear require attending-defined progression gates.
Lachman test is more sensitive than anterior drawer for ACL insufficiency in the acute setting.
Lateral femoral condyle and posterior tibial plateau bone bruise pattern on MRI supports ACL injury mechanism.

metadata.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from acl tear despite appropriate initial management
metadata.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

metadata.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after acl tear treatment
metadata.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones

metadata.trigger: ACL tear planned for reconstruction with limited preoperative motion
metadata.action: Prioritize prehab to regain extension and reduce arthrofibrosis risk before surgery
